subject: Public Health
title: Indigenous Health Disparities
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Indigenous Health Disparities.

hook.text: Indigenous peoples in every country have worse health outcomes than their non-Indigenous neighbors.

buildup.text: Colonization, forced displacement, and cultural destruction created health disparities that persist for generations.

discovery.text: Aboriginal Australians live 8 years less than non-Indigenous Australians. In Canada, the gap is similar.

twist.text: Indigenous-led health programs—rooted in traditional knowledge and community control—produce better outcomes.

climax.text: Closing the gap requires addressing historical trauma, land rights, and self-determination—not just healthcare.

punchline.text: Health disparities are the scars of colonization. Healing requires justice.

quiz.question: What produces the best health outcomes for Indigenous communities?
options: Indigenous-led programs rooted in community control
